title: Autonomy Phase 1: Internal signal plumbing (timer → runtime → planner)
description:
## Source
`Data/Pages/Tasks/memorysmith_llm_autonomy_design_2026-06-29.md` — Phase 1

## Scope
Wire all non-chat triggers into the same runtime path. Timer emitter using `ITimeProvider`, signal sink, wake logging, deduplication, suppression cooldowns.

## Deliverables
- Signal envelope type
- Runtime signal sink
- Timer emitter using `ITimeProvider`
- Wake logging (reason, goal ID, accepted/suppressed)
- Wake deduplication
- Suppression cooldowns

## Exit criteria
- Timer wakes can be simulated in tests
- A timer wake can reach the planner
- No duplicated planning on repeated timer ticks

comments.author: anonymous
comments.body:
Council review update (2026-06-29): Phase 1 scope expanded to include:
1. `IRuntimeSignalSink` + timer signal entry point — moved to TSK-0249
2. Suppression cooldowns with named boolean gates
3. Wake deduplication (identical reason within cooldown)
4. Structured wake logging (reason, goal ID, accepted/suppressed, suppression reason)
5. Minimum-viable dashboard: wake reason display + pause toggle — moved to TSK-0248
6. Search dedup/caching required before timer-driven autonomy — create separate task

Dependency chain: Phase 0 contracts must ship first (TSK-0238 + TSK-0243 + TSK-0247).

See full report: `Data/Pages/council/blueprint-yaml-autonomy-council-2026-06-29.md`
